context: now we've actually pulled the source out, not just trimmed the edges. Bring me that and I'll sleep easy for t
full_text: I want to be sure they don't come back. There's a {quest_item} I need. It'll let me know we've actually pulled the source out, not just trimmed the edges. Bring me that and I'll sleep easy for the first time in weeks.
note: natural spoken contrast with metaphor, character emphasizing thoroughness

match: , not when I'm old and telling the story.
context: something this big. I won't forget that, not when I'm old and telling the story.
full_text: We did it. We actually did it, and it's better than I let myself imagine. Take {reward_item}, and know that you helped me believe I could finish something this big. I won't forget that, not when I'm old and telling the story.
note: natural 'not when...' idiom, sincere emphasis not corrective reframing

match: , not in {other_settlement}, not anywhere I know of.
context: at that level. Not in {settlement_name}, not in {other_settlement}, not anywhere I know of.
full_text: You've heard of {target_npc}'s reputation, then? Good. Then you understand why I'm willing to send a stranger across the region just for a conversation. There's no one else within reach who's at that level. Not in {settlement_name}, not in {other_settlement}, not anywhere I know of.
note: natural escalating spoken list of places, emphatic not corrective

context: , too. I owe you for both pieces of this, not just the one.
full_text: Good. That should make the walk safer for the rest of us, too. I owe you for both pieces of this, not just the one.
note: natural spoken emphasis, clarifying what they're thanking for

match: , not a trap, and you watch.
context: u set out bait that looks like a mistake, not a trap, and you watch.
full_text: Stores going missing with nothing broken? I had the exact same problem two seasons ago. It drove me half mad until I figured out the trick. You set out bait that looks like a mistake, not a trap, and you watch.
note: describing the trick itself, the contrast is the mechanic of the advice

context: and I think the accusation was the lever, not the cause. I haven't said it out loud because I do
full_text: There's a piece I haven't told anyone yet. The person who first pointed at my friend had reasons of their own to want them gone, and I think the accusation was the lever, not the cause. I haven't said it out loud because I don't have proof, but I will by the time this is over.
note: metaphor distinction, character reasoning through their own theory aloud

context: I can't bring this to her myself. Not yet. I need you to find {target_npc} in {tar
full_text: I can't bring this to her myself. Not yet. I need you to find {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}. They used to know us both, before all this. If anyone can tell her I'm trying, it's them.
note: natural spoken hesitation fragment, completely idiomatic

context: hat she heard. I never meant it that way, not even close. But I see now how it sounded, and I can
full_text: {target_npc} actually told you? Oh. Oh, that's what she heard. I never meant it that way, not even close. But I see now how it sounded, and I can't unsay it.
note: natural spoken emphatic denial, 'not even close' is pure idiom

match: Not all the way through, but more than he has in weeks.
context: He slept last night. Not all the way through, but more than he has in weeks. He's not back yet, and I don't know if
full_text: He slept last night. Not all the way through, but more than he has in weeks. He's not back yet, and I don't know if he's going to be the same when he is, but he's reachable now, and that's everything. Please take {reward_item}, offered by a wife who had run out of hope and has some back, because you brought my husband one step closer to home.
note: natural qualification, wife honestly tempering the good news

context: She needs something given back, not more taken away. Something small that says the world isn
full_text: She needs something given back, not more taken away. Something small that says the world isn't done with her yet.
note: natural antithesis in spoken advice, describing what she needs

match: Not everyone wants to be tangled up in another person's grief.
context: Of course. Not everyone wants to be tangled up in another person's grief. I'll be all right either way.
full_text: Of course. Not everyone wants to be tangled up in another person's grief. I'll be all right either way.
note: 'Not everyone' starts a full sentence here, natural observation not pre-emption

match: I used to tell my son that story to make him careful. Now
context: s were old enough to remember properly. I used to tell my son that story to make him careful. Now I wonder if I made him afraid of the wro
full_text: His grandfather was killed by {enemy_type_plural} too, before any of us were old enough to remember properly. I used to tell my son that story to make him careful. Now I wonder if I made him afraid of the wrong things.
note: genuine temporal reflection, not a rhetorical then/now pivot

match: , not my failing mind, just a creature with poor manners.
context: ms, droppings in the corner. Not a ghost, not my failing mind, just a creature with poor manners. Now I need to seal the gap.
full_text: There it is. Scratches on the beams, droppings in the corner. Not a ghost, not my failing mind, just a creature with poor manners. Now I need to seal the gap.
note: character is working through the possibilities she actually feared, relief reading

match: Not because I don't believe in it.
context: e thought about stopping more than once. Not because I don't believe in it. Because it gets quiet doing something n
full_text: I suppose the honest part is that I've thought about stopping more than once. Not because I don't believe in it. Because it gets quiet doing something nobody thanks you for. But then the winter comes and the stores are there, and that's answer enough.
rewrite: I suppose the honest part is that I've thought about stopping more than once. It gets quiet doing something nobody thanks you for. Then the winter comes and the stores are there, and that's answer enough.
note: textbook causal-flip 'Not because X. Because Y.' pattern called out in R14; cut the scaffolding

context: done the first time. Support from behind, not from in front.
full_text: Perfect. I'll hand it to my cousin and step back. That's what I should have done the first time. Support from behind, not from in front.
note: natural terse spoken restatement of the lesson learned

match: , not the twine and prayer I've been using.
context: _item} for the repairs. The real repairs, not the twine and prayer I've been using. If I can get the perimeter solid again
full_text: I need {gather_count} {quest_item} for the repairs. The real repairs, not the twine and prayer I've been using. If I can get the perimeter solid again I might actually sleep tonight.
note: character-voiced idiom 'twine and prayer', self-deprecating spoken contrast

match: , not the kind I've been doing with rope and optimism.
context: e repairs. The kind of repair that lasts, not the kind I've been doing with rope and optimism. Bring me that and the road opens up aga
full_text: I need a {quest_item} to make the repairs. The kind of repair that lasts, not the kind I've been doing with rope and optimism. Bring me that and the road opens up again.
note: character voice, 'rope and optimism' is an authentic spoken phrase

context: s rebuilt, the formulas are tested, and I can work again without second-guessing every measureme
full_text: The archive is rebuilt, the formulas are tested, and I can work again without second-guessing every measurement. Take {reward_item}, the first thing I made with my hand steady and my formulas back where they belong. You helped me recover something that fire couldn't kill, only scatter.
note: plain statement of resumed capability, no corrective reframing present

context: l would say no. Too expensive, too risky, not enough demand. So I'm building it on my own plot with
full_text: You're wondering why I didn't mention this to the council first. Because {settlement_npc} told me the council would say no. Too expensive, too risky, not enough demand. So I'm building it on my own plot with my own materials, and I'll present them with a working kiln instead of a proposal they can reject.
note: listing the anticipated objections, natural spoken parallel

context: because I wanted you to see the ambition, not the longing.
full_text: You saw the drawings on the wall, didn't you. Those aren't just plans. They're dreams I've been sketching since I was twelve years old, sitting in the back of someone else's forge and wondering if I'd ever have my own. I didn't tell you that because I wanted you to see the ambition, not the longing.
note: introspective vulnerability, genuine distinction the speaker is owning

context: rms? That's coordinated hunting behavior, not scavenging. From the bite patterns you're describin
full_text: Livestock kills across multiple farms? That's coordinated hunting behavior, not scavenging. From the bite patterns you're describing, I'd say you have a pack that's shifted its territory. Hit them where they rest during the day and hit them together.
note: expert distinguishing two real categories, natural in-character technical speech

match: , not pretty, not finished, but standing.
context: The workshop is standing, not pretty, not finished, but standing. Take {reward_item}. I found one jar in
full_text: The workshop is standing, not pretty, not finished, but standing. Take {reward_item}. I found one jar in the rubble that came through without a crack, and I've been keeping it on the new shelf because it reminds me what you did. You showed up when the ground was still shaking, and that's the kind of thing a person remembers for a long time.
note: spoken rhythmic qualification with ring-compositional return to 'standing', authentic

context: at's gone makes you feel it more clearly, not less. Now comes the part I've been holding in
full_text: I set it at the edge of where the oldest tree used to stand. Funny how marking what's gone makes you feel it more clearly, not less. Now comes the part I've been holding in my teeth for months.
note: character's honest observation about grief, natural 'not less' emphasis

context: ld. I am not watching that happen twice. Not with her garden.
full_text: I want {kill_count} of those {enemy_type_plural} gone. They'll come back otherwise, and they'll do to the new growth what they did to the old. I am not watching that happen twice. Not with her garden.
note: spoken fragment with emotional weight, grief-driven emphasis

context: ars and nothing came up. Not thin growth, not poor yield. Nothing. I need a {quest_item} to test
full_text: I planted in the same field I've used for twelve years and nothing came up. Not thin growth, not poor yield. Nothing. I need a {quest_item} to test the soil and find out whether the ground has given up on me or whether something else is at work.
note: farmer working through disbelief, naming the possibilities he would have expected

context: Solved. Actually solved, not patched, not postponed. The channel holds, the shelves are full
full_text: Solved. Actually solved, not patched, not postponed. The channel holds, the shelves are full, and the road is safe. I keep waiting for the next thing to fail and it hasn't.
note: authentic spoken emphasis of relief after long struggle, character can't believe it

context: ost beautiful thing I've heard in weeks. Not cured, I know. But managed means she stays.
full_text: {target_npc} said it can be managed? That word, managed, is the most beautiful thing I've heard in weeks. Not cured, I know. But managed means she stays.
note: natural spoken self-acknowledgment, tempering their own hope

match: Not talking yet, but working, which is his way of talking.
context: He's at the workbench this morning. Not talking yet, but working, which is his way of talking. {reward_item} is yours, handed over by
full_text: He's at the workbench this morning. Not talking yet, but working, which is his way of talking. {reward_item} is yours, handed over by a brother who had nothing to offer his own brother for weeks and is finally able to offer something to someone. You helped me reach a person I couldn't reach alone, and that's a debt I'll carry gladly.
note: natural qualification with tender specificity about a brother's character

match: Not the same, but she'd have understood the gesture.
context: That's close. Not the same, but she'd have understood the gesture. I put it where hers used to sit, and th
full_text: That's close. Not the same, but she'd have understood the gesture. I put it where hers used to sit, and the room feels less empty now.
note: natural qualification, gentle honest acknowledgment that the item isn't her lost one

match: , not one of the best, the best.
context: st {self_role} this region has ever seen, not one of the best, the best. That starts with materials, and I need
full_text: I've decided I'm going to be the best {self_role} this region has ever seen, not one of the best, the best. That starts with materials, and I need {gather_count} {quest_item} of the finest quality I can get. No excuses, no substitutions.
note: in-character bravado with authentic repetition for emphasis

context: item} to fund the proposal I'm bringing. Not gifts, investments. I want the council to see that I've alr
full_text: I need {gather_count} {quest_item} to fund the proposal I'm bringing. Not gifts, investments. I want the council to see that I've already started the work, that I'm not asking for the seat, I'm showing them I've already been sitting in it.
note: political-climber bravado, crisp asserted correction of expectation fits the character voice already established in the template

context: That trader was the one moving goods out, not some child.
full_text: I'd swear to it in front of anyone. That trader was the one moving goods out, not some child.
note: natural witness-voice emphasis, pointing at the right culprit

context: when I said it and the words were chosen, not thrown. That's the part that doesn't fit the ve
full_text: You picked up on something I left out, didn't you. I called it an argument, like we were both fighting, but I wasn't angry. I was calm when I said it and the words were chosen, not thrown. That's the part that doesn't fit the version I've been telling, because there was no heat and I just wanted to win.
note: honest self-reckoning, the contrast is the insight the character is finally admitting

match: , not just feeling bad about it.
context: it shows I'm serious about making amends, not just feeling bad about it. The next step is harder.
full_text: That's a good {quest_item}. It won't undo anything, but it shows I'm serious about making amends, not just feeling bad about it. The next step is harder.
note: natural spoken clarification of intent, not AI pre-emption

context: kept alive by stubbornness at this point, not by any real grievance. The older generation holds the line out
full_text: {target_npc} says the feud is mostly kept alive by stubbornness at this point, not by any real grievance. The older generation holds the line out of habit, and the younger ones follow because nobody's given them permission to stop. That gives me hope, thin hope, but real.
note: natural analysis of the situation, genuine distinction between cause types

context: an frame it as an addition to the circle, not a break from tradition. Bring me a {quest_item} they've made so
full_text: The person I love makes a particular kind of {quest_item} that nobody in the trade circle produces. If I can show my family that this person brings a skill we're missing, I can frame it as an addition to the circle, not a break from tradition. Bring me a {quest_item} they've made so I can present it.
note: diplomatic framing the speaker is planning to use, natural distinction

context: en through {other_settlement} recently. That's not nothing. That's an opening, and I've been too s
full_text: So {target_npc} says this person has mentioned me more than once, and always warmly. They asked about my work, my health, whether I'd been through {other_settlement} recently. That's not nothing. That's an opening, and I've been too scared to step through it.
note: 'that's not nothing' is a pure spoken idiom, completely natural

context: Aye, that's fair. It's my ghost, not yours. I'll carry it a while longer.
full_text: Aye, that's fair. It's my ghost, not yours. I'll carry it a while longer.
note: natural spoken resignation, simple binary contrast owning their burden

context: ares. She doesn't need to know different, not yet. Just help me finish what I started, fri
full_text: I've been fortifying the house for months now, and I'm close to having it done. I need {gather_count} {quest_item} to finish the last of it, and my hands are shaking too much lately to go gather it myself. My wife thinks I'm out checking snares. She doesn't need to know different, not yet. Just help me finish what I started, friend, and I'll tell you the rest after.
note: natural spoken 'not yet' qualification, character protecting wife

full_text: It's old business. What matters is finishing it, not dressing it up with stories.
